name: SuperAnnotate ML Data Annotation API
what_it_does: Enterprise ML data annotation platform with REST API and Python SDK for creating, managing, and automating annotation workflows for computer vision (images, video) and NLP (text, documents) datasets — with active learning and quality assurance features.
use_cases: Uploading images and triggering automated pre-annotation with existing models
Managing annotation projects and assigning tasks to annotators programmatically
Exporting annotated datasets in COCO, Pascal VOC, and custom formats for training
Implementing active learning loops by querying uncertainty scores and triggering re-annotation
Monitoring annotation quality metrics and inter-annotator agreement via API
not_for: Simple one-time labeling jobs without programmatic management needs
Real-time inference annotation (use Roboflow for simpler workflows)
Text-only annotation at small scale (use Label Studio for simplicity)
best_when: An ML team needs enterprise annotation workflows with automation, quality assurance, and active learning integration for large-scale computer vision or NLP datasets.
avoid_when: You need a simple annotation tool for small datasets — SuperAnnotate targets enterprise ML teams with complex workflows.

auth.notes: API key from SuperAnnotate dashboard. Passed as Authorization header. Team-scoped access. Python SDK handles auth automatically with key. Separate keys for different team access levels.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 3 users, limited items per month
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $200/month; Business from $600/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: User and annotation volume based pricing. Managed annotation services available as add-on. Competitive with Labelbox for enterprise. Python SDK is primary integration method.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits documented per endpoint; bulk operations have higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python SDK is primary interface — SDK documentation more complete than REST docs. REST API available for non-Python agents. Error responses include codes and messages. Webhook delivery for project events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Item upload is idempotent by name within project. Annotation exports are idempotent. Task assignment operations are not idempotent — check assignment status before assigning.
known_agent_gotchas: Python SDK is primary interface — REST API has less coverage than SDK
Annotation exports can take minutes for large projects — use async export pattern
Image upload to cloud storage required before annotation items can be created
Quality assurance workflows have specific state machine — understand states before automating

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. API key auth. TLS enforced. Data stored in customer's cloud storage (S3, GCS) — platform only stores metadata. Strong data sovereignty model.
